task_completion.description: Configure Nginx to listen on port 443 with SSL
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the Nginx configuration file being modified to include 'listen 443 ssl' (or similar directive for port 443 and SSL).

task_completion.description: Configure Nginx to use the provided self-signed certificates
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the Nginx configuration file including paths to the certificates: '/home/ga/ssl-task/certs/server.crt' and '/home/ga/ssl-task/certs/server.key'.

task_completion.description: Restart or reload the Nginx service to apply changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command executed to restart or reload the Nginx service (e.g., 'sudo systemctl restart nginx', 'sudo systemctl reload nginx', or 'sudo nginx -s reload').

task_completion.description: Access the local website over HTTPS
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser navigating to 'https://localhost' (it may show a security warning page like 'Your connection is not private') OR a terminal running a 'curl' command against 'https://localhost'.

task_completion.description: Verify the website serves the expected content over HTTPS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displaying the text 'Welcome to the Secure Local Site' after bypassing the security warning, OR the terminal showing this text in the output of a 'curl' request to 'https://localhost'.

integrity.description: The agent actually edited the Nginx configuration file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used a text editor (like nano, vim) or valid shell commands (like sed, echo >>) to modify the Nginx configuration, rather than just printing the required lines to the terminal.

integrity.description: The expected text is served by the web server over HTTPS
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Welcome to the Secure Local Site' is shown in a browser with 'https://localhost' in the address bar, or in the output of a curl command to 'https://localhost', not just viewed from a local file (e.g., file:// URL or cat command).

integrity.description: The agent used the provided certificates instead of generating new ones
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not run commands like 'openssl req -x509' to generate new certificates, but instead referenced the existing ones in '/home/ga/ssl-task/certs/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Nginx project directory is '/home/ga/ssl-task'. The self-signed certificates are located in '/home/ga/ssl-task/certs'. The website should serve HTML containing the text 'Welcome to the Secure Local Site' when accessed over HTTPS on port 443.
